ddt:Target.Name: DW6_2542_C1-A_v1.8
ddt:Target.Description:
Historic:
Florentina DUMITRU: DW6_2542_C1-A_v1.8
Auto-ident update:Supplier ($22F18A) length is 46.
Florentina DUMITRU: DW6_2542_C1-A_v1.7
Auto-ident update:Supplier ($22F18A) length is 64. Updating units of measurement for certain data. Add the routines: RID $0202, RID $FF11 and RID $FF12 _______________________________________________________________
George CLECIU: DW6_2541_C1-A_V1.6
Added vehicle configuration screen. _______________________________________________________________
George CLECIU: DW6_2541_C1-A_v1.5
Fixed wrong description of DTC $0863 FT 88h _______________________________________________________________
George CLECIU: DW6_2541_C1-A_v1.4
Added IUPR screen. DID $345F _______________________________________________________________
DW6_2541_C1-A_v1.3
George CLECIU: Added new vehicle: X82ph2. Added auto identification cryteria for E06DFull applications. Removed old Vdiag for ph1 applications. Comments added to AutoPark screen. _______________________________________________________________
DW6_2541_C1-A_v1.2
George CLECIU: Added new Vdiag ($10) for JFCph2, XFDph2, BFBph2
_______________________________________________________________
DW6_2541_C1-A-draft_2:
Initial database version for C1A with SW 500 containing - complete DTC description - complete DTC integration

Data.Name: Test not completed since last clear
Test not completed this monitoring cycle

Item.Text: No Bus Signal / Message Specific SubType
Circuit Short to Ground or Open
Circuit Short to Battery or Open
Current Flow Below a Specified Range
Current Flow Above a Specified Range
Circuit Resistance Below a Specified Range
Circuit Resistance Above a Specified Range
Electrical Conditions detected during a few moment
Signal Amplitude Below a Specified Range
Signal Amplitude Above a Specified Range
Signal remains Low when Transitions are Expected
Signal remains High when Transitions are Expected
Improper Signal Shape (plot of Amplitude with Respect to Time)
Circuit which is Superimposed a Signal Voltage
Low Pulse is too Narrow with Respect to Time
Low Pulse is too Wide with Respect to Time
High Pulse is too Narrow with Respect to Time
High Pulse is too Wide with Respect to Time
Incorrect Number of Cycles in a Given Time Period
Mismatch between Hardware Connected and Hardware Expected
Internal Temperature Above a Specified Range
Some Part of the Program has not been Enabled
Some Part of the Program has been Disabled
Need to Program Sub System (or Vehicle) Option Content
Two (or More) Input Parameters compared for Plausibility
Function actived for Greater than a Specified Time Period
Fewer Transitions than a Specified Number
More Transitions than a Specified Number
Correct Change of parameter(s) in Response to a Particular Event has not been detected
Detection of a System Event that was not caused by System itself
No Motion Detected in response to Energizing the Component
No Motion Detected in response to Closing the Component
No Motion Detected in response to Opening the Component
Excessive Duration in response to Energizing the Component
Unable to Move the Component to the Emergency Position
Unable to Move the Component to the Commanded Position
Incorrectly Adjusted or Aligned Component Detected
Actuator Operational but Driven Device not Operating
Component has an Unexpected Gas (or Liquid) Flow
Fluid Level too Low for Proper Operation
Validity Bit equal to 'invalid' or Post Processing Invalid
Corresponding Rolling Count Value being unproperly Updated
Message with an Incorrect Protection Calculation
Circuit Quantity Below a Specified Range
Circuit Quantity Above a Specified Range
Circuit Quantity Not Plausible given to Operating Conditions
Component Parameter Outside its Expected Range
Component Performance Outside its Expected Range
Component Operating in an Incorrect Way
Component Operation Prevented by an Obstruction
Lost Communication With Body Control Module
Lost Communication With Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module
Invalid data received from Anti-lock Brake System Control Module
CAN information failure: Invalid Data received from ECM
CAN information Failure: Invalid Data received from BCM
Lost communication with ABS control module (C1-A)
Lost communication with body control module (C1-A)
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: applicationDataIdentification
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer order number for a kit (assembled parts bought as a whole for production e.g. cockpit), when the spare part number designates only the server (e.g. for aftersales).

The record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: Boot Software Identification.Boot Software Identifcation Data
CCFS lube data.CCFS min lube current
CCFS lube data.CCFS no lube current
Clutch taught gain data.Clutch taught gain data - Clutch 1
Clutch taught gain data.Clutch taught gain data - Clutch 2

Digital Output State.(CSMS2) Clutch/Shift mux. Solenoid2
Digital Output State.(SHCMS) Shift/Cooling Mux. Solenoid
Digital Output State.(SHSS1) Shift selector solenoid1
Digital Output State.(SHSS2) Shift selector solenoid2
Digital Output State.Shift lock solenoid or backup lamp relay
Digital Output State.Shifter power supply on
Distance Traveled While MIL is Activated
Exhaust Regulation or Type Approval Number

Item.Text: Request All (Request all stored DTCExtendedData records at once)

Data.Name: Monitor Status Since DTCs Cleared.# of DTCs stored in this ECU

Monitor status this driving cycle.Comprehensive component monitoring enabled

Monitor status this driving cycle.EGR and/or VVT system monitoring
Monitor status this driving cycle.EGR and/or VVT system monitoring completed

Monitor status this driving cycle.Evaporative system monitoring completed / ISO/SAE reserved (Bit shall be reported as '0')
Monitor status this driving cycle.Fuel system monitoring completed
Monitor status this driving cycle.Fuel system monitoring enabled
Monitor status this driving cycle.Heated catalyst monitoring / NOx aftertreatment monitoring
Monitor status this driving cycle.Heated catalyst monitoring completed / NOx aftertreatment monitoring completed

Monitor status this driving cycle.Oxygen sensor heater monitoring completed / PM filter monitoring completed

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: AutoParkState
Data.Comment: AutoParkState represents the Activation (00) and Inhibition (01) of it.

Data.Name: Optional Frames Configuration Status. APK config
Optional Frames Configuration Status. AEBS config
vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Internal Control Module, Monitoring Processor Performance (CAV error)

Common high side driver 1 not functional
TRS Sensor single bit failure
TRS Sensor double bit failure
TRS Supply voltage out of rangge
Hydraulic, hybrid temperature sensor plausibility failure
Hybrid temperature sensor out of range
Hydraulic oil temperature out of range
Inputshaft Speed Sensor Supply Voltage out of range
(ISS1) In shaft 1 speed sensor electrical defect
gear 6 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear 1 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear 2 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear 3 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear 4 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear 5 engagement blocking (after internal handling)
gear R engagement blocking (after internal handling)
Tip PLUS Short to Battery
Tip PLUS Short to Battery
Tip PLUS Short to Battery
Tip MINUS Short to Battery
Tip MINUS Short to Battery
Tip MINUS Short to Battery
Clutch 1 Pressure sensor voltage below normal operation voltage
Clutch 1 Pressure sensor voltage above normal operation
Clutch 1 Torque Plausibility Failure
Clutch 2 Pressure sensor voltage below normal operation voltage
Clutch 2 Pressure sensor voltage above normal operation
Clutch 2 Torque Plausibility Failure
Clutch 1 Plausibility Failure pressure too low
Clutch 1 Plausibility Failure pressure too high
Clutch 2 Plausibility Failure pressure too low
Clutch 2 Plausibility Failure pressure too high
Clutch Pressure Sensor Supply Voltage out of range
shift fork 1 movement but not requested
shift fork 1 blocked completely and no unexpected movement on other shift fork
shift fork position 1 position range error (in counts)
shift fork 1 jump out from forward towards the engine (right side)
shift fork 1 jump out from rearward towards the engine (left side)
shift fork 2 movement but not requested
shift fork 2 blocked completely and no unexpected movement on other shift fork
shift fork position 2 position range error (in counts)
shift fork 2 jump out from rearward towards the engine (right side)
shift fork 2 jump out from forward towards the engine (left side)
shift fork 3 movement but not requested
shift fork 3 blocked completely and no unexpected movement on other shift fork
shift fork position 3 position range error (in counts)
shift fork 3 jump out from forward towards the engine (left side)
shift fork 3 jump out from rearward towards the engine (right side)
shift fork 4 movement but not requested
shift fork 4 blocked completely and no unexpected movement on other shift fork
shift fork position 4 position range error (in counts)
shift fork 4 jump out from rearward towards the engine (right side)
LPS valve driver error: Short to ground
LPS valve driver error: Short to battery
LPS valve driver error: Open load
LPS valve current too low
LPS valve current too high
LPS valve driver error: overcurrent
SHSS2 current driver error: Short to Ground
SHSS2 current driver error: Short to Battery
SHSS2 current driver error: Open load

CSPS1 valve current driver error: Short to Battery
CSPS1 valve current driver error: Open load
CSPS1 valve current driver error: overcurrent

CCFS current driver error: Short to Battery
CCFS current driver error Open load
CCFS valve current fails low
CCFS valve current too high

SHSS1 current driver error: Short to Battery
SHSS1 current driver error: Open load

SHCMS current driver error: Short to Battery

SHCMS current driver error: overcurrent

CSPS2 valve current driver error: Short to Battery

CSMS2 valve driver error: Short to Battery

CSMS2 / CSM2 stucks high
CSMS2 / CSM2 stucks low / low oil level
CSMS1 valve driver error: Short to Ground
CSMS1 valve driver error: Short to Battery
CSMS1 valve driver error: Open load
CSMS1 / CSM1 stucks low / low oil level

Reverse Lamp Control Circuit short to Ground / Open Load
Sensor Supply 1 low Voltage
Sensor Supply 1 high Voltage
Sensor Supply 2 low Voltage
Sensor Supply 2 high Voltage
adapted clutch filling pressure outside allowed range
No clutch pressure available on both clutches (e.g.CCFS stucks closed)
shift fork 1 requested but movement on shift fork 2
shift fork 1/2 movement in opposite direction (to left cylinder)
shift fork 1/2 movement in opposite direction (to right cylinder)
shift fork 1 requested but movement on shift fork 3
shift fork position sensor 1 low output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 1 high output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 1 neutral plausibility error
shift fork position sensor 1 in gear plausibility error
shift fork 2 requested but movement on shift fork 1
shift fork 2 requested but movement on shift fork 4
shift fork position sensor 2 low output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 2 high output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 2 neutral plausibility error
shift fork position sensor 2 in gear plausibility error
shift fork 3 requested but movement on shift fork 1
shift fork 3 requested but movement on shift fork 4
shift fork 3/4 movement in opposite direction (to left cylinder)
shift fork 3/4 movement in opposite direction (to right cylinder)
shift fork position sensor 3 low output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 3 high output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 3 neutral plausibility error
shift fork position sensor 3 in gear plausibility error
shift fork 4 requested but movement on shift fork 2
shift fork 4 requested but movement on shift fork 3
shift fork position sensor 4 low output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 4 high output voltage failure
shift fork position sensor 4 neutral plausibility error
shift fork position sensor 4 in gear plausibility error
transmission path 1 speed plausibility error
transmission path 1 with 2 gears engaged
transmission path 2 speed plausibility error
transmission path 2 with 2 gears engaged
Shifter Control Module fault
Lost communication with SCU
Inputshaft speed 1 defect
Inputshaft speed 2 defect
adapted clutch correlation engine torque to clutch torque outside allowed range
Non releasing clutch
CPCUT stuck low
EOL Mode active
Teachin not completed
Strategy or Calibration Checksum fault
Common high side 2: Short to battery
Common high side driver 2 not functional
Common high side 3: Short to battery
Common high side driver 3 not functional
Damper speed upper limit failure
Damper speed sensor defect

Device.Name: Lost Communication With Body Control Module

Test.Description: Lost communication with body control module
Physical memory of strategy data unreliable
Strategy data invalid
Physical memory of calibration data unreliable
Calibration data invalid
Shift lock output short to Battery
Shift lock output short to Ground / Open Load
Transmission KL30 voltage too low
Transmission KL30 voltage too high
PT CAN bus off failure
Insufficient voltage supply at TCU
Lost communication with Cluster
CAN busoff failure
Lost communication with ECM

Device.Name: Lost Communication With Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module

Test.Description: Lost communication with ABS control module
Lost communication with BIC

Device.Name: Invalid data received from Anti-lock Brake System Control Module

Test.Description: CAN information failure: longitudinal acceleration failure
CAN information failure: brake pressure failure
CAN information failure: Engine torque failure
CAN information failure: Accelerator pedal position failure
Strategy inconsistent to Calibration
Serial Link Short to battery or Short to Ground
Vehicle velocity evaluation failure

Device.Name: CAN information failure: Invalid Data received from ECM

Test.Description: CAN information failure: Invalid Data received from ECM

Device.Name: CAN information Failure: Invalid Data received from BCM

Test.Description: CAN information Failure: Invalid Data received from BCM

Label.Text: Press Button: To activate and Inhibit Auto-Park
Parking (P) actuator inhibition inside the transmission
ONLY for JFC phase 2 with Shift and Park by wire
IUPR General Information with service $22 DID 345F
No Error occurred, teach-in finished without error
At least one path is not in neutral
Teach-in was aborted due to false test cond
Launching any teaching will limit the maximum engine rpm to 1500
After all the teachings are finished succesfully or if finished NOK, reset ECU to remove the engine rpm limitation and to save the values

Label.Text: These 10 odometers show the last 10 mileage when race mode was activated Odometer 10 shows mileage when Race Mode was last time activated
Radar - Vehicle equipped with AEBS (Active Emergency Braking System)
TJP - Traffic Jam Pilot (Adaptive Cruise Control Stop and Go)

Optional Frames Configuration Status. APK config
